question: This area chart shows average housing prices ($1000s) across different cities. Which statement best describes the visualization?

options.A: It reveals a clear overall pattern across the cities, suggesting an underlying continuous progression.
options.B: It inappropriately uses a continuous filled area across separate cities, giving a false impression of a smooth trend between them.
options.C: Denver has the highest average housing price among all the cities shown.
options.D: Portland has the lowest average housing price among all the cities shown.

explanation: This area chart joins category values for city into a continuous filled region. However, the x‑axis categories (e.g., Portland, Austin, Boston, Denver, ...) have no inherent numeric or spatial continuity, so the apparent smooth trend is an artifact of the chart type rather than a meaningful pattern. A bar chart would allow readers to compare each category independently without implying a continuous progression.
